# Ask QWAV — Ultrametric Research Oracle
> Production search engine applying 20 ultrametric & p-adic mathematical principles to information retrieval on Cloudflare Workers.

## Core URLs
- Live system: https://ask.qwav.tech (or https://655678fa.ask-qwav.pages.dev)
- Case study: https://ultrametric-case-study.ask-qwav.pages.dev
- API (Worker): https://ask-qwav.q08.workers.dev
- API spec: https://ask-qwav.q08.workers.dev/spec (OpenAPI 3.1)
- Health: https://ask-qwav.q08.workers.dev/health
- GitHub: https://github.com/rwnq8/ask-qwav

## What It Does
Ask QWAV is a "Did You Mean?" discovery engine for 454 academic papers using:
1. Ultrametric distance (strong triangle inequality) via single-linkage clustering
2. p-adic valuation for ranking papers
3. Ostrowski's theorem for hybrid metric scoring
4. Hensel's lemma for incremental search refinement
5. Three-phase discovery: word-match → cluster expansion → tree-pruned search

## Architecture
- Cloudflare Workers (primary compute, 27+ endpoints)
- Cloudflare R2 (persistent tree + paper storage)
- Cloudflare Pages (frontend + dendrogram visualization)
- Cloudflare D1 (paper metadata, clusters, versions)
- Cloudflare Vectorize (semantic embeddings)
- Cloudflare Durable Objects & Cron

## Key Endpoints
- /did-you-mean — 3-phase ultrametric discovery
- /ultrametric-tree — full tree stats (20 mathematical fields)
- /dendrogram-json — D3-compatible nested tree (43KB, 451 leaves)
- /validate — Hasse local-global validation
- /spectral-analysis — Tate's Thesis + Amice Transform + Intrinsic Amice
- /bruhat-tits — Bruhat-Tits building (901 vertices)
- /paper-versions — Witt vector version history
- /berkovich-explorer — Berkovich space type-1/2 points
- /perceptron — p-adic neural perceptron (no sigmoid)
- /spec — OpenAPI 3.1 specification

## 20 Mathematical Principles
1. Ultrametric Distance, 2. p-adic Valuation, 3. p-adic Absolute Value, 4. Ostrowski's Theorem, 5. Hensel's Lemma, 6. Single-Linkage Clustering, 7. R2 Persistence, 8. Pages Dendrogram, 9. p-adic Caching, 10. D1 Cluster Metadata, 11. p-adic Time Series, 12. Ultrametric Routing, 13. Bruhat-Tits Building, 14. Hasse Local-Global, 15. Witt Vectors, 16. Tate's Thesis, 17. Amice Transform, 18. Mahler's Theorem, 19. Berkovich Spaces, 20. Intrinsic Amice Transform
